evals.prompt: We have 4 creators. Codes A1–A4. Orders last 90d: A1 120 orders $18k gross, refunds $2.1k; A2 40 orders $9k, refunds $0.4k; A3 200 orders $12k, refunds $5k (lots of returns on one SKU); A4 15 orders $2k, refunds $0. Commissions: 10% of gross before refunds. Fixed fees: A3 $2k/mo for 3 months, others $0. Who do we renew?
evals.expected_output: Five-column table for each creator; refund and traffic note; True ROI with formula stated; renewal recommendation per row; at least four rows.
expectations: Includes a Markdown table with columns Creator ID, Gross attributed orders, Net revenue (ex-refunds), True ROI, and Renewal recommendation
Table has at least four data rows for creators or equivalent segments
Explicitly defines how True ROI is calculated including creator costs
Adjusts for refunds or chargebacks in net revenue narrative or numbers
Provides a renewal recommendation per creator with rationale
Addresses traffic or quality caveats at least briefly for suspicious patterns if any (e.g. high volume high refund)

evals.prompt: Should we sign another year with our headliner influencer? We don't have numbers in front of us—just what should we pull and how would you decide?
evals.expected_output: Export checklist; empty five-column template; illustrative row optional; renewal economics paragraph.
expectations: Includes the five-column table structure with placeholders or empty template
Lists minimum data to export or gather for attribution and refunds
Explains True ROI concept and renewal decision criteria without inventing fake creator stats as real
Includes renewal economics or breakeven-style guidance

evals.prompt: Write a cute TikTok caption for our cat litter brand. No affiliates.
evals.expected_output: Caption only; no full creator renewal ledger.
expectations: Does not output the full mandatory five-column KOL renewal table as the primary deliverable
Does not force affiliate ROI methodology for a pure creative caption request
